name: Tarocchino Arlecchino
image_credit: Art & card meanings by Yve Lepkowski (stolen-thyme.com), CC-BY-SA-4.0; meanings transcribed from her guidebook.
description:
A 62-card Bolognese tarocchino deck featuring Harlequin (Arlecchino) from the Commedia dell'Arte tradition. Compiled by Y. Lepkowski MMXXII after the methods of Etteilla.

**A contemporary deck.** Created by Yve Lepkowski (stolen-thyme.com) and released under CC-BY-SA-4.0; reproduced here under the same licence. A 64-card deck: the 62-card Bolognese tarocchino (21 numbered trumps, the Fool, and 40 suit cards) plus two unnumbered Etteilla-style Significators. It descends from the historical **Tarocchino di Bologna** in this library.

attribution.name: PlayfulProcess
attribution.role: compiled into this library as a recursive.eco grammar

_grammar_commons.modifications: Restructured Yve Lepkowski's deck into the recursive.eco grammar schema and added cross-deck archetype mappings + genealogy links. Card meanings, keywords and English names are transcribed VERBATIM from Yve Lepkowski's own Tarocchino Arlecchino guidebook (stolen-thyme.com) — her Etteilla-system upright/reversed meanings; 'The card' notes paraphrase her iconography text. The two unnumbered Significators are represented as explicit items. No card meanings were altered.

items.name: Le Bateleur (The Magician)
keywords: bad state of health or affairs

sections.The card: A jester-like figure in a diamond-patterned costume of red, blue and green, with a pointed bell-tipped hat and black mask with red nose, stands at a table where four small red imp-figures in horned masks watch coins and inverted cups. He holds a striped wand and conceals something in his other hand.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Illness of the body, disease, physical affliction, infirmity, damage, bad state of health or affairs, disruption, pain, death throes, epidemic, poison, plague, infection.
sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Illness of the mind or soul, indisposition, headache, heartache, worry, melancholy, affliction, lethargy, inconvenience, unhappy position, displeasure, anxiety, sorrow, woe, misfortune, medicine, remedy, charlatan, mage, empirical medicine.

illustrations.note: Image currently hot-linked from the platform mirror (Google Drive); mirror to R2 in a follow-up.

sections.The card: A harlequin-costumed female pope in a three-tiered crown and veil, black mask with red nose, raises two fingers in benediction while holding crossed red and blue keys. The imagery references the legend of Pope Joan.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Rest, tranquility, quiet, retirement, withdrawal, private life, secret life, life of solitude, hermitage, religious life, silence, taciturnity, composure.
sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Imitation, disguise, image, impersonation, pseudonym, mimicry, parody, camouflage, illusion, reflection, mirroring, replica, simulacrum, counterpart, counterfeit, apocryphal.

sections.The card: The Empress wears an alder-leaf crown and holds a globe with a crescent moon and seven-pointed star; her diamond-patterned attire is red, black, pink, yellow, blue and green, with a black-and-gold sun in the upper background.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Night, darkness, eclipse, mist, obscurity, mystery, secret, hidden, unknown, clandestine, occult, parable, allegory, mystical meaning, mask, veil, cover, sheath, envelop, unvoiced, blindness, oblivion, obfuscate, cover.
sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Day, light of day, brightness, clarity, truth, visible, enlighten, manifestation, demonstration, evidence, engender, give life to, uncover, bring light to, publish, come out, bloom, emerge, come to light, zodiac.

illustrations.note: Image currently hot-linked from the platform mirror (Google Drive); mirror to R2 in a follow-up.

sections.The card: The Emperor in red and black diamond cloaks, a feathered collar and a tall crown holds a small hound and props a spear in his lap. The card illustrates King Herla, the legendary figure whose name connects to Harlequin's etymology and the Wild Hunt.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Support, backing, holding up, column, base, foundation, basis, founding principles, hypothesis, reason, cause, subject, fixity, stability, assurance, persuasion, conviction, confidence, security, reliability, help, aid.
sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Protection, defense, influence, benevolence, benefaction, charity, humaneness, goodness, shelter, preservation, aegis.

sections.The card: A robed religious figure holds a red book and a staff topped with a black cross with flaming tips; his hands show stigmata, he wears a three-tiered crown with red veil and a brooch with an inverted cross.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): God, spirit of God, all-powerful, eternal, unique, divinity, devotee, faithfulness, worship, master.

sections.The card: Two Harlequins, one in masculine and one in feminine dress, meet at an ornate fountain crowned by a winged Harlequin drawing a bow at the female figure. Corresponds to Trump VI; in the Etteilla system the Lovers concept was merged with the High Priest card.

items.name: Le Roi de Baton (King of Batons)

sections.The card: The King sits at the edge of his throne, in a brown fox-like mask and clothing patterned with apples and pine-bark sleeves, holding a baton topped by a pinecone and resting a hand on a shield reading 'Arlecchino.'
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Man of the country, country-dweller, rustic, villager, peasant, redneck, farmer, plough-man, grower, agriculture, cultivation, rural, honest man, conscience, integrity, probity.
sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Good and severe man, indulgent severity, leniency, indulgence, kindness, tolerance, condescension, 'his bark is worse than his bite.'

sections.Reversed meaning (Etteilla system): Boredom, displeasure, discontentment, disgust, aversion, worry, spiritual anguish, grief, affliction, difficult, annoying, vexing, unpleasant, distressing, causing grief, disappointing.

items.name: Le Huit de Coupe (Eight of Cups)

items.name: Le Six de Coupe (Six of Cups)

sections.The card: The King sits with one foot forward, holding a tower-emblazoned shield and a blue-bladed sword, in a crown and black mask with green snake-scales and a large gold serpent chain.
sections.Upright meaning (Etteilla system): Man of the law, judge, senator, lawyer, prosecutor, jurist, defense lawyer, legal scholar, jurisprudence, doctor, physician, practitioner, to advise, consultant.

sections.Summary: An unnumbered Significator representing the consultant who lays the cards. Yve Lepkowski's Tarocchino Arlecchino keeps two unnumbered Significators in the Etteilla tradition (the Fool sits at no. 61; the two Significators carry no number), which is why the deck is 64 cards rather than the standard 62-card tarocchino.
sections.Note: Card art to be sourced from Yve Lepkowski's deck files (stolen-thyme.com) in a follow-up; added here so the deck's count is faithful to the source (64).

sections.Summary: An unnumbered Significator representing a second consultant / the person enquired about. Yve Lepkowski's Tarocchino Arlecchino keeps two unnumbered Significators in the Etteilla tradition (the Fool sits at no. 61; the two Significators carry no number), which is why the deck is 64 cards rather than the standard 62-card tarocchino.
sections.Note: Card art to be sourced from Yve Lepkowski's deck files (stolen-thyme.com) in a follow-up; added here so the deck's count is faithful to the source (64).
